name: Basil
notes: 400x Herb Basil Thai Liquorice Flavour Seeds
growing_instructions: 400x Herb Basil Thai Liquorice Flavour Seeds

name: Basil
notes: 400x Herb Basil Thai Liquorice Flavour Seeds
growing_instructions: 400x Herb Basil Thai Liquorice Flavour Seeds

name: Parsley
notes: Classic curly parsley with finely crinkled leaves. Italian variety with robust flavour.
growing_instructions: Soak seed overnight to aid germination. Sow direct outdoors or start indoors in late winter. Germination 14-21 days (slow). Thin to 20cm apart. Can be slow to establish. Harvest outer leaves regularly once plant is well established. Biennial producing mainly leaves in first year.

name: Dill
notes: 1000x Dill Seeds
growing_instructions: Sow direct outdoors in spring, barely cover seeds. Germination 7-14 days. Thin to 20cm apart. Prefers well-drained soil. Water moderately. Can self-seed for next season. Harvest leaves throughout growing season. Leave some plants to flower for seed heads for pickling.

name: Sage
notes: Broad-leaved sage variety with excellent flavour. Perennial herb producing for many years.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in spring on surface of seed compost, lightly cover. Germination 10-14 days. Prick out into individual pots. Plant out after frost 60cm apart in well-drained soil. Drought tolerant once established. Perennial producing foliage for many years. Harvest leaves as required.

name: Thyme
notes: Creeping thyme with purple-tinged foliage. Ground cover variety with typical thyme flavour.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in spring on surface of seed compost, lightly cover. Germination 10-14 days. Prick out into individual pots. Plant out 30cm apart in well-drained soil. Creeping variety works well as ground cover. Drought tolerant once established. Perennial producing foliage for many years. Harvest stems as required for culinary use.

name: Parsley
notes: Large-leaved Italian flat parsley with robust flavour. Prolific producer with 4000 seeds for multiple sowings.
growing_instructions: Soak seed overnight to aid germination. Sow direct outdoors or start indoors in late winter. Germination 14-21 days (slow). Thin to 20cm apart. Can be slow to establish. Succession sow for continuous supply. Harvest outer stems regularly once plant established. Biennial producing mainly leaves in first year.

name: Chives
notes: Fine-leafed herb with delicate onion flavour. Early spring perennial producing throughout season.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in late winter on surface of seed compost, lightly press in. Germination 7-14 days. Prick out into individual pots. Plant out in clumps 25cm apart in moist soil. Perennial producing fine foliage throughout year. Harvest leaves regularly as required. Purple flowers in late spring attract insects.

name: Lemongrass
notes: Tropical aromatic herb with lemony flavour. Popular in Asian cuisine and for herbal teas.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in spring on moist seed compost, lightly cover. Germination 14-21 days at 21-25°C (slow). Prick out into individual pots. Grow on in warm location. Plant out after all frost risk in warm sunny spot. Alternatively grow in containers indoors. Harvest stems as required for culinary use.

name: Peppermint
notes: 500x Peppermint Mentha Piperita Herb Seeds
growing_instructions: 500x Peppermint Mentha Piperita Herb Seeds

name: Oregano
notes: Culinary oregano with excellent flavour for Mediterranean cooking. Compact perennial herb.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in spring on surface of seed compost, lightly cover. Germination 7-14 days at 15-20°C. Prick out into individual pots. Plant out after last frost 30cm apart in well-drained soil. Drought tolerant once established. Perennial producing foliage for many years. Harvest leaves as required for drying or fresh use.

name: Thyme
notes: Common thyme variety with fine foliage and typical culinary thyme flavour. Hardy perennial ground cover.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in spring on surface of seed compost, lightly cover. Germination 10-14 days at 15-18°C. Prick out into individual pots. Plant out 30cm apart in well-drained soil. Drought tolerant when established. Compact growth suitable as border edging. Perennial producing foliage for many years. Harvest stems as required for culinary use.

name: Basil
notes: Organic sweet basil with classic Italian flavour. Ideal for pesto and Mediterranean cooking.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in spring on surface of seed compost, lightly cover. Germination 7-10 days at 18-21°C. Prick out into individual pots. Plant out after last frost in warm sunny spot, 30cm apart. Pinch out flower buds and growing tips to encourage bushier growth. Harvest leaves regularly throughout season. Can be grown in containers.

name: Lettuce
notes: A fast-growing blend of 6 different loose-leaf cutting lettuce varieties. A special blend of light green, dark green, and red lettuce of different textures and shaped leaves. Perfect grown as baby leaves, giving up to four ‘cuts’, or equally good grown out as more mature leaf lettuces. | Sow directly outside between March-August in prepared soil.; Sow the seeds 1.5cm deep in shallow rows and lightly cover.; Water as needed and germinate will occur in 7-14 days depending on temperature.; Thin to 5cm apart when large enough to handle. Use the leaves when thinning for baby leaves; Water regularly to prevent the plants from bolting.; Harvest the leaves regularly by picking a few leaves from each plant.; From sowing, harvest baby leaves in around 20 days.; Sow every 4 weeks for a continuous supply of fresh lettuce.
growing_instructions:
Days To Germination: 7-14 days
Optimum Soil Temp. For Germination: 4-21°C
Days to Harvest: 30-75 days
Planting Depth: 0.5cm
Spacing, Seed: 0.5cm
Spacing, Row: 30-45cm
Spacing, Plant: 15-20cm
Light: Sunny Location

name: Lettuce
notes: Lettuce ‘Crispy Mix’ is a blend of crisp-leaf varieties, including Sulfita, Nadita, Crispita 2, and Crackita. This mix delivers crunchy, tender leaves with a range of vibrant green and red shades, perfect for salads, sandwiches, or as a fresh garnish. | Sow directly outside between March-August in prepared soil.; Sow the seeds 1.5cm deep in shallow rows and lightly cover.; Water as needed and germinate will occur in 7-14 days depending on temperature.; Thin to 5cm apart when large enough to handle. Use the leaves when thinning for baby leaves; Water regularly to prevent the plants from bolting.; Harvest the leaves regularly by picking a few leaves from each plant.; From sowing, harvest baby leaves in around 20 days.; Sow every 4 weeks for a continuous supply of fresh lettuce.
growing_instructions: Sow directly outside March-August in prepared soil 1.5cm deep in shallow rows. Germination 7-14 days. Thin to 5cm apart. Harvest baby leaves in around 20 days. Water regularly to prevent bolting.

name: Mustard
notes: Salad Leaf ‘Tasty Mustard Mix’ features a vibrant, flavourful combination of mustard varieties, including Pizzo, Red Lace, Red Zest, Red Lion, and Green Frills. This mix is ideal for adding a spicy kick to salads and other culinary dishes. | Sow indoors all year round.; Alternatively, sow directly outside between May-September.; Sow the seeds on moist compost and lightly cover.; Use the leaves when thinning for baby leaves; Water regularly to prevent the plants from bolting.; Harvest the leaves regularly when they are 12-15cm by picking a few leaves from each plant.; From sowing, harvest baby leaves in around 20 days and mature plants in 35 days.
growing_instructions:
Mustard Salad Leaf Tasty Mix – Key Facts
Sowing Months Indoors: All Year
Sowing Months Outdoors: May-September
Days To Germination: 7-10 days
Optimum Soil Temperature: 15c+
Plant Height: 10-15cm
Plant Spacing: 5-10cm
Harvesting Months: May-October
Light: Full sun

name: Carrot
notes: This British bred F1 Nantes hybrid has good resistance to the carrot root fly. With top quality cylindrical roots, these carrots have good colour and skin quality. | Best sown straight in the ground and then thinned in stages to obtain the correct distance apart. Never plant in cold or weedy soil as carrots are difficult to weed once established.; Carrots are cool-weather vegetables, so start sowing from about two weeks before the last expected frost in your area.; Make successive plantings every three weeks but avoid the hottest part of the summer.; Sow in drills about 2cm deep and 15cm apart. With this spacing the foliage of adjacent plants will make a dense canopy when the plants are mature.; Sow the seeds sparingly and cover with about 0.5cm of soil. Seeds must be kept moist to germinate.; When sowing seeds, try to space them 1cm apart. The tiny seeds make spacing difficult, but it will be easier to thin without disturbing the plants you plan to leave, if there is a little space between them. Seeds can be mixed with sand to make sowing a little easier.; You can try mixing radish seeds with the carrot seeds. The carrot seeds are slow to germinate, and the radishes, which germinate and grow very quickly, will mark the row until the carrots come up and provide another crop.; A second crop of carrots can be planted in late summer or early autumn in most areas. If a hard frost threatens, protect your autumn crop with a heavy mulch.
growing_instructions:
Type:  Hardy Annual
Time to Germinate: 14-21 Days
Temperature Required: 12°c +
Days to Maturity: 70-80 Days
Light: Full Sun

name: Carrot
notes: A superb new Nantes type with a more intense deep orange colour and exceptional taste delivering high yields of uniform cylindrical shaped roots with round tips. Allotment growers favourite. | Dig or till the carrot bed deeply when the soil is on the dry side to avoid making lumps.; Work the soil to a fine texture 15-25 cm deep to allow the carrot roots to grow long and shapely.; Avoid freshly-manured soil, which may produce hairy, rough roots and will cause forks and splits;; Sow seed thinly in rows, 1 cm deep, 30-40 cm between the rows as soon as the danger of hard frost has passed. Try to get about 4 seeds per 2 cm.; Thin to 16-20 carrots per 30 cm for fresh eating whilst small and 6-10 carrots per 30 cm for mid season crops, depending on the root size you want, and keep weeded and watered.; Dispose of thinned seedlings to avoid attracting Carrot Fly.; As they grow, push soil up over any exposed roots to prevent a green shoulder.
growing_instructions:
Carrot Sweet Candle F1 – Key Facts
Type:  Hardy Annual
Time to Germinate: 14-21 Days
Temperature Required: 12°c +
Days to Maturity: 70-80 Days
Light: Full Sun

name: Carrot
notes: A unique mixed combination of colour and taste sensations that are sure to excite and delight at the dinner table. Purple has a smooth skin and coreless orange flesh which is sweet and tasty and especially striking when sliced. Yellow holds its bright sunny hue inside and out, is extra crunchy and juicy and has a wonderfully refreshing sweet flavour. White is very mild and especially delicious when young. Red has very high lycopene levels as well as a crispy texture that is great when cooked. | Purple – Cosmic Purple, Yellow – Solar Yellow, White – Lunar White, Red – Atomic Red.  (Mixed Seed Packet of equal quantities); Please note carrots are not the easiest of vegetables to grow well and benefit from lighter stone free soils.; Dig or till the carrot bed deeply when the soil is on the dry side to avoid making lumps.; Work the soil to a fine texture 15-25cm (6-10 in.) deep to allow the carrot roots to grow long and shapely.; Avoid freshly-manured soil, which may produce hairy, rough roots and will cause forks and splits;; Sow seed thinly in rows, 1cm (1/2 in.) deep, 30-40cm (12-16 in.) between the rows as soon as the danger of hard frost has passed. Try to get about 4 seeds per 2cm (1 in.).; Thin to 16-20 carrots per 30 cm (1ft.) for fresh eating whilst small and 6-10 carrots per 30 cm (1ft.) for mid season crops, depending on the root size you want, and keep weeded and watered.; Dispose of thinned seedlings to avoid attracting Carrot Fly.; As they grow, push soil up over any exposed roots to prevent a green shoulder.
growing_instructions:
Type:  Hardy Annual
Time to Germinate: 14-21 Days
Temperature Required: 12°c +
Days to Maturity: 70-80 Days
Light: Full Sun

name: Pepper
notes: Pepper ‘Habanero Chocolate’ produces lantern-shaped fruits in rich, deep brown tones. Known for its intense heat and smoky, earthy flavour, it adds depth and complexity to sauces, salsas, and marinades. The plants can reach 3ft but take longer to mature than others, so best started early. These can be sometimes difficult to germinate if conditions are not ideal, but well worth the effort. Being perennial they can be over-wintered inside to produce an early crop the following year. 🌶️ SCOVILLE HEAT RATING: 300,000 – 575,000 units | Sow the seeds on moist soil and lightly cover.; Gently compress the soil and place it in a bright location.; These are best in a heated propagator, at a constant temperature of 22-24c.; Water as necessary and the seeds should germinate between 10-21 days.; Once true leaves have developed, transplant the seedlings into individual pots.; When 10-12cm plant in a greenhouse or conservatory with the root ball 1cm below the surface.; The stem may require some support.; Water regularly and feed weekly with our all-purpose plant feed.
growing_instructions:
Sowing Months: Mid December-March
Days To Germination: 10-21 days
Optimum Soil Temperature: 22-24c
Plant Height: 60-90cm
Light: Full sun

name: Pepper
notes: Pepper ‘Habanero Orange’ is a classic hot chilli producing small, lantern-shaped fruits in vibrant orange. Compared to other habanero varieties, it has a fruitier flavour while still delivering intense heat. Perfect for adding fiery spice to sauces, salsas, and marinades. | Sow seeds 1/4″ deep in 3in pots with quality seed compost. Keep moist.; Seedlings emerge in 10-21 days at 70degrees F+.; Transplant to individual containers when seedlings have at least two pairs of leaves.; When 4-5 inches plant 18″ apart in greenhouse / conservatory with root ball 1/2″ below the soil surface.; Keep moist and feed when flowering with tomato type fertiliser.; Harvest when fruits have reached full-size and full colour.
growing_instructions:
Sowing Months: Mid December-March
Days To Germination: 10-21 days
Optimum Soil Temperature: 22-24c
Plant Height: 60-90cm
Light: Full sun

name: Tomato
notes: Tomato Cherry Sweet Million F1. A very heavy cropping F1 cherry variety producing huge amounts of cherry sized extra sweet bright red fruits.  A robust variety it will perform well under glass or outdoors in a sunny sheltered location.  Height 2m & Spread 50cm. | Sow in spring 1/16 inch deep. Germination takes around 6-14 days at 65-75F.; Transplant the seedlings when large enough to handle into 3 inch pots.; Grow on under cooler conditions and when about 8 inches tall, either plant in their growing position in the greenhouse or gradually acclimatise them to outdoor conditions.; Plant out 18 inches apart in a warm and sunny spot in moist, fertile well drained soil and keep watered.
growing_instructions: Sow spring 1/16 inch deep. Germination 6-14 days at 18-24°C. Transplant when 8 inches tall. Plant 18 inches apart in warm, sunny, moist soil. Support stem.

name: Tomato
notes: A high yielding cherry variety, delivering an abundance of 10-15g bright red small fruits with an abundance of flavour with high levels of both sugar & acid, which combine for an exceptional refreshing tang. | Sow in spring 1/16 inch deep.; Germination takes around 6-14 days at 65-75F.; Transplant the seedlings when large enough to handle into 3 inch pots.; Grow on under cooler conditions and when about 8 inches tall, either plant in their growing position in the greenhouse or gradually acclimatise them to outdoor conditions.; Plant out 18 inches apart in a warm and sunny spot in moist, fertile well drained soil and keep watered.
growing_instructions: Sow in spring 1/16 inch deep. Germination 6-14 days at 18-24°C. Transplant when large enough to handle into 3 inch pots. Grow on under cooler conditions. When 8 inches tall, plant in greenhouse or gradually acclimatise to outdoors. Plant 18 inches apart in warm sunny spot in moist fertile well drained soil.

name: Tomato
notes: Tomato ‘Nagina F1’ is the first truly blight-resistant plum tomato variety, producing excellent crops of small, 65gm fruits with rich, full flavour. Perfect for salads or cooking, this reliable variety is ideal for both greenhouse and outdoor growing. | Sow in spring 1/16 inch deep in fine seed grade compost.; Germination takes around 6-14 days at 65-75F.; Transplant the seedlings when large enough to handle into 3 inch pots.; Grow on under cooler conditions and when about 8 inches tall, either plant in their growing position in the greenhouse or gradually acclimatise them to outdoor conditions and plant out 18 inches apart in a warm and sunny spot in moist, fertile well drained soil and keep watered.
growing_instructions: Sow in spring 1/16 inch deep in fine seed grade compost. Germination 6-14 days at 18-24°C. Transplant when large enough into 3 inch pots. Grow on under cooler conditions. When 8 inches tall, plant in greenhouse or gradually acclimatise to outdoors. Plant 18 inches apart in warm sunny spot in moist fertile well drained soil.

name: Tomato
notes: An outstanding indeterminate mini-plum variety delivering huge trusses of bright red, dense and firm sweet fruits. Will need support and best under glass. RHS Award of Garden Merit. | Sow in spring 1/16 inch deep in fine seed grade compost.; Germination takes around 6-14 days at 65-75 F.; Transplant the seedlings when large enough to handle into 3 inch pots.; Grow on under cooler conditions and when about 8 inches tall, either plant in their growing position in the greenhouse or gradually acclimatise them to outdoor conditions and plant out 18 inches apart in a warm, sheltered and sunny spot in moist, fertile well drained soil and keep watered.
growing_instructions: Sow in spring 1/16 inch deep in fine seed grade compost. Germination 6-14 days at 18-24°C. Transplant when large enough into 3 inch pots. Grow on under cooler conditions. When 8 inches tall, plant in greenhouse or gradually acclimatise to outdoors. Plant 18 inches apart in warm sheltered sunny spot in moist fertile well drained soil. Will need support.

name: Tomato
notes: Tomato ‘Supersweet 100 F1’ is a medium-early variety known for its heavy yields and exceptionally sweet flavour. It produces long, trailing trusses packed with small, round fruits that can weigh up to 20g each, with up to 100 tomatoes on a single truss under ideal conditions. This F1 hybrid grows well both indoors and out, offering reliable crops over a long season and consistently high eating quality straight from the vine. Indeterminate. | Sow the seeds on moist soil and lightly cover.; Gently compress the soil and place it in a bright location.; These are best in a heated propagator, at a constant temperature of 20-24c.; Water as necessary and the seeds should germinate between 7-14 days.; Once true leaves have developed, transplant the seedlings into individual pots.; Pinch out the seedlings when they have 6 sets of leaves.; The stem may require some support.; Water regularly and feed weekly with our all-purpose plant feed.
growing_instructions:
Sowing Months: February-April
Days To Germination: 7-14 days
Optimum Soil Temperature: 18c+
Plant Height: 250cm+
Plant Spacing: 60-90cm
Light: Full sun

name: Tomato
notes: Tomato ‘Romello F1’ is an outdoor cherry plum variety with late blight resistance and sweet, flavourful fruit. It produces long clusters of small, juicy tomatoes with good resistance to cracking. A compact bush variety, it is ideal for growing in hanging baskets, containers, or small garden spaces. | Sow the seeds on moist soil and lightly cover.; Gently compress the soil and place it in a bright location.; These are best in a heated propagator, at a constant temperature of 20-24c.; Water as necessary and the seeds should germinate between 7-14 days.; Once true leaves have developed, transplant the seedlings into individual pots.; If planting in hanging basket, place one per basket.; Water regularly and feed weekly with our all-purpose plant feed.
growing_instructions:
Sowing Months: February-April
Days To Germination: 7-14 days
Optimum Soil Temperature: 18c+
Plant Height: 30cm
Plant: Light: Full sun

name: Cucumber
notes: An excellent F1 Hybrid Gherkin size cucumber suitable for both outdoor and under glass production. The fruits produced are small, light green, ribbed with a small number of warts, perfect for pickles and preserves. This productive plant has high resistance to downy Mildew and green mottle mosaic virus. In main season the fruits will need picking on almost a daily basis. Self pollinating variety. | Best sown under cover (protected / heated) from Mid spring, 3-4 seeds 1in deep in a 3in pot in good compost, and thin later to the best one or two plants per clump. (These will not germinate in cold / damp conditions, use a propagator or warm draft free windowsill); Transplant no later than 3 weeks after seeding as cucumbers have a tap root that can be easily damaged.; Cucumbers germinate and grow best when summer really gets going, and will not appreciate being planted out too early unless protected with fleece.; To avoid bitterness, keep plants evenly moist at all times.; Can be trained on a trellis to save space or left to roam.; Cut fruits regularly to encourage long continuous production.
growing_instructions:
Cucumber Gherkin Anulka F1 – Key Facts
Type: Half-Hardy Annual
Temperature Required: 21-25°c
Time to Germinate: 7-14 Days
Days to Maturity: 50 – 60 Days
Position/ Light: Full Sun

name: Pepper
notes: A medium to tall variety producing heavy crops of pungent, thick walled dark green peppers which ripen to deep red. (Fruit 8cm x 2cm with blunt end).  2,000 – 5,000 SHU’s. | Sow seeds 1/4″ deep in 3in pots with quality seed compost. Keep moist. Seedlings emerge in 10-21 days at 70degrees F+.; Transplant to individual containers when seedlings have at least two pairs of leaves.; When 4-5 inches plant 18″ apart in greenhouse / conservatory with root ball 1/2″ below the soil surface.; Keep moist and feed when flowering with tomato type fertiliser.  Harvest when fruits have reached full-size and full colour.
growing_instructions: Sow 1/4" deep in 3in pots with seed compost, keep moist. Emerge 10-21 days at 21°C+. Transplant to individual containers at 2+ pairs of leaves. Plant 4-5 inches high, 18" apart, 1/2" below soil. Keep moist, feed with tomato fertiliser when flowering. Harvest full-size and full colour.

name: Pepper
notes: Pepper Hot ‘Large Red Cherry’ is a productive variety that produces round, cherry-sized fruits that ripen from green to a glossy, vibrant red. Thick-walled and full of flavour, these compact peppers deliver a spicy kick, making them ideal for pickling or adding heat to a wide range of dishes. 🌶️ SCOVILLE HEAT RATING: 2,500 – 5,000 units | Sow the seeds on moist soil and lightly cover.; Gently compress the soil and place it in a bright location.; These are best in a heated propagator, at a constant temperature of 22-24c.; Water as necessary and the seeds should germinate between 7-14 days.; Once true leaves have developed, transplant the seedlings into individual pots.; The stem may require some support.; Water regularly and feed weekly with our all-purpose plant feed.
growing_instructions:
Days to Germination: 10-21
Optimum Soil Temp. for Germination: 70F+
Days To Harvest: 70-90
Planting Depth: 1/4in
Spacing, Plant: 24 inches
Light: Greenhouse / Conservatory

name: Parsnip
notes: 350x Parsnip White Gem Seeds
growing_instructions: 350x Parsnip White Gem Seeds

name: Onion
notes: 300x Jumbo Sweet Spanish Onion Seeds
growing_instructions: 300x Jumbo Sweet Spanish Onion Seeds

name: Leek
notes: Winter hardy leek producing tall white stems. Excellent for autumn and winter harvesting. Very cold tolerant.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in late winter into seed compost. Germination 12-18 days. Plant out seedlings when 15-20cm tall into prepared soil, 15cm apart with 30cm between rows. Gradually earth up around stems as plants grow. Harvest from October through to March by digging from ground.

name: Rhubarb
notes: Spring and autumn rhubarb variety producing tender pink stems with excellent flavour. Perennial producing for many years.
growing_instructions: Soak seed overnight. Sow into pots of seed compost indoors. Germination 14-21 days. Grow on until strong seedlings, then transplant outdoors. Space 60cm apart in deep, fertile soil enriched with compost. Do not pull stems first year. Subsequently pull stems from April onwards. Perennial plant produces for 10+ years.

name: Lettuce
notes: Mini romaine lettuce producing compact heads with tender leaves. Classic variety for home gardeners.
growing_instructions: Sow direct outdoors or start in trays indoors. Germination 7-10 days. Thin seedlings to 20cm apart. Keep soil moist throughout growing season. Ready to harvest 8-10 weeks after sowing. For continuous supply, sow seeds every 2-3 weeks from spring to early autumn.

name: Onion
notes: Bunching onion producing long white stems with mild flavour. Early maturing variety for spring and summer harvest.
growing_instructions: Sow direct in spring, 1cm deep in rows 15cm apart. Thin seedlings to 10cm. Alternatively sow indoors in late winter and transplant. Germination 10-14 days. Keep well watered. Harvest when stems reach desired thickness, typically 12-16 weeks after sowing. Can be harvested continuously by picking outer stems.

name: Broccoli
notes: Large green broccoli heads on compact plants. Classic calabrese variety with excellent flavour.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in spring or direct sow in summer. Germination 7-10 days. Thin to 45cm apart. Keep well watered throughout growing season. Harvest main head while tight, secondary heads will develop. Ready to harvest 60-70 days after transplanting.

name: Cabbage
notes: Large round red cabbage with sweet flavour. Simple to grow variety ideal for home gardeners.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in early spring for summer cabbage, or late spring for autumn/winter harvest. Germination 7-10 days. Transplant seedlings 45cm apart each way. Water regularly and keep weed-free. Harvest when heads are firm, typically 60-90 days after transplanting depending on season.

name: Kale
notes: Large dark purple-black leaves with excellent texture. Borecole kale variety improves flavour after frost.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in late spring for autumn/winter harvest. Germination 7-12 days. Transplant when 15cm tall, 45cm apart. Prefers cool weather and improves in flavour after frost. Harvest outer leaves regularly from October through to spring. Can produce for several months.

name: Radish
notes: Fast growing mix of red, purple and yellow radish varieties. Ready to harvest in just 25-30 days.
growing_instructions: Sow direct in shallow drills, 1cm deep, thinning to 5cm apart. Successive sowing every 2 weeks for continuous supply. Germination 3-7 days. Keep soil moist for crisp roots. Ready to harvest 25-30 days after sowing. Pull when marble-sized to avoid woodiness.

name: Aubergine
notes: F1 hybrid aubergine producing prolific dark purple fruits. Reliable heavy cropper suitable for greenhouse or outdoors.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in early spring in heated propagator at 21-25°C. Germination 7-14 days. Prick out into individual pots. Plant out into greenhouse or warm sheltered spot when 30cm tall. Space 60cm apart. Provide support. Pinch out growing tip to encourage branching. Harvest fruits when young and glossy.

name: Rocket
notes: Cultivated rocket with peppery flavour. Prolific producer with plenty of seeds for repeated sowings.
growing_instructions: Sow direct outdoors in spring and continue sowing every 2-3 weeks for continuous supply. Germination 5-10 days. Thin seedlings to 15cm apart. Keep moist but not waterlogged. Ready to harvest in 30-40 days. Cut leaves when young and tender. Can be harvested continuously.

name: Pak Choi
notes: Chinese cabbage with white stems and tender green leaves. Fast-growing brassica ready in 6-8 weeks.
growing_instructions: Sow direct or start indoors in spring. Germination 7-10 days. Thin to 25-30cm apart. Keep well watered for tender leaves. Can be harvested whole or pick outer leaves. Early maturing variety ready 6-8 weeks after sowing. Succession sow for continuous supply.

name: Leek
notes: Winter hardy leek producing large white stems. Extended harvest season from autumn through to spring.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in late winter into seed compost. Germination 12-18 days. Transplant seedlings into garden when 15-20cm tall, 15cm apart with 30cm between rows. Earth up regularly to blanch stems. Harvest from October through to March by lifting from ground.

name: Shallot
notes: Red and white striped bulbs with excellent flavour. Prolific producer dividing into multiple bulbs.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in late winter into seed compost. Germination 7-14 days. Transplant seedlings into garden when 10-15cm tall, 15cm apart in rows 30cm apart. Bulb division starts in summer. Harvest when foliage yellows, typically July/August. Cure in warm dry place before storing.

name: Carrot
notes: Nantes type carrot with slender cylindrical roots and excellent sweet flavour. Reliable heavy cropper.
growing_instructions: Sow direct in well-prepared fine seedbed, 1.5-2cm deep in rows 25-30cm apart. Thin to 7-10cm when large enough. Keep weed-free and watered. Succession sow every 2 weeks for extended harvest. Ready to harvest from June onwards. Bold shoulders and good colour.

name: Spinach
notes: Winter hardy spinach variety producing large tender leaves. Excellent for autumn and winter harvesting.
growing_instructions: Sow direct outdoors in late summer for autumn/winter harvest. Germination 7-14 days. Thin seedlings to 15cm apart. Keep soil moist. Can be harvested continuously by picking outer leaves or whole plants. Frost hardy, improves in flavour after frost. Successive sowings July-August for extended harvest.

name: Spinach
variety: Giant Winter Baby Leaf Year Round
notes: Winter spinach baby leaf variety for year-round cultivation. Fast maturing tender leaves for salads.
growing_instructions: Sow direct in shallow drills outdoors. Germination 7-14 days. Thin to 10cm apart for baby leaf production. Keep soil consistently moist. Harvest when leaves reach tender stage approximately 30-40 days after sowing. Succession sow every 2-3 weeks for continuous supply.

name: Courgette
notes: F1 hybrid courgette producing prolific yellow and green striped fruits. Early maturing variety suitable for pots and borders.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in spring in pots, 1-2 seeds per pot. Germination 6-10 days at 20-25°C. Thin to strongest seedling. Plant out after last frost with careful pot spacing 60cm apart in warm spot. Keep soil moist but not waterlogged. Feed weekly once flowering starts. Harvest fruits regularly when 15-20cm for continuous production.

name: Lettuce
notes: Red loose-leaf lettuce with frilly deeply lobed leaves. Attractive and tender variety for salads.
growing_instructions: Sow direct outdoors or start indoors in trays. Germination 7-10 days. Thin seedlings to 20-25cm apart for full-sized plants or 10cm for baby leaves. Keep soil moist. Ready to harvest 8-10 weeks after sowing. Harvest outer leaves continuously or cut whole head. Succession sow every 2-3 weeks for continuous supply.

name: Lettuce
notes: Mixed salad leaf blend for cut-and-grow-again harvesting. Easy to cultivate blend of tender leaves.
growing_instructions: Sow direct in shallow drills or broadcast over prepared soil. Germination 5-10 days. Do not thin - cut whole crop when leaves reach 10-15cm height. Regrows for multiple harvests. Keep soil moist. Succession sow every 2-3 weeks for continuous harvest. Ideal for containers and small spaces.

name: Lettuce
notes: Heritage butterhead lettuce suitable for cultivation throughout year. Reliable variety with soft buttery leaves.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in late winter for spring crop or direct outdoors spring-autumn. Germination 7-10 days. Thin to 20-25cm apart. Keep soil consistently moist. Ready to harvest 60-70 days after sowing. Cut heads when firm and full. Can be sown successively for year-round supply.

name: Lettuce
notes: Green loose-leaf salad bowl lettuce with deeply lobed tender leaves. Prolific producer ideal for repeated harvesting.
growing_instructions: Sow direct outdoors or start indoors in trays. Germination 7-10 days. Thin to 20cm apart for full plants or 10cm for baby leaves. Keep consistently moist to prevent bolting. Harvest outer leaves continuously starting 6-8 weeks after sowing. Multiple harvests from single plant. Succession sow every 2-3 weeks.

name: Lettuce
notes: Red loose-leaf salad bowl lettuce with deeply lobed tender leaves. Prolific producer ideal for repeated harvesting.
growing_instructions: Sow direct outdoors or start indoors in trays. Germination 7-10 days. Thin to 20cm apart for full plants or 10cm for baby leaves. Keep consistently moist to prevent bolting. Harvest outer leaves continuously starting 6-8 weeks after sowing. Multiple harvests from single plant. Succession sow every 2-3 weeks.

name: Mint
notes: 800x Mint Seeds
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in spring on surface of seed compost, lightly cover. Germination 7-14 days. Prick out into individual pots. Plant out 30cm apart in moist soil. Vigorous spreader, may need containment. Perennial producing foliage for many years. Harvest leaves regularly throughout growing season for fresh use or drying.

name: Coriander
notes: Cilantro/coriander herb variety with both leaf and seed uses. Fast-growing annual popular in Asian and Mexican cooking.
growing_instructions: Sow direct outdoors in spring through autumn in succession. Germination 7-10 days. Thin to 15cm apart. Prefers cool seasons, tends to bolt in heat. Keep soil consistently moist. Harvest leaves throughout season for fresh use. Leave plants to flower for coriander seed production.

name: Gherkin
notes: 20x Gherkin F1 Adam Pickling Gherkin Cucumber Seeds
growing_instructions: 20x Gherkin F1 Adam Pickling Gherkin Cucumber Seeds

name: Asparagus
notes: Classic asparagus variety producing thick tender spears. American heirloom reliable for home gardeners, yields for 15+ years.
growing_instructions: Soak seed overnight before sowing. Sow indoors in spring 1cm deep in seed compost. Germination 14-21 days. Prick out into deep pots (6 inches). Plant out when 3-4 months old into permanent beds 30-45cm apart. Do not harvest first two years. From year 3, harvest spears 15-20cm tall from April through June. Cut below soil surface with knife.

name: Gherkin
notes: Vigorous pickling gherkin cucumber with prolific small fruits. Traditional variety excellent for pickles and preserves.
growing_instructions: Sow under cover in spring, 1-2 seeds 1cm deep per pot. Germination 6-10 days at 20-25°C. Thin to strongest seedling. Transplant carefully 3 weeks after sowing. Space 60cm apart in warm sunny spot. Keep soil moist but not waterlogged. Pinch out growing tips to encourage branching. Harvest fruits daily when 6-8cm for best quality and continuous production.

name: Beetroot
notes: Early beetroot producing uniform globe-shaped roots with excellent deep red colour. Sweet flavour, quick maturing variety.
growing_instructions: Sow direct outdoors from March to July in drills 1.5-2cm deep, 30cm between rows. Thin seedlings to 10cm when large enough. Keep weed-free and watered. Avoid excessive nitrogen. Ready to harvest 50-70 days after sowing when roots are golf-ball to tennis-ball sized. Leave in ground until needed or lift and store in cool, dark place.

name: Lettuce
notes: Classic iceberg variety producing solid, dense heads with crisp pale leaves. Great Lakes type bred for reliability and storage.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in late winter for spring crop or direct sow spring through early autumn. Germination 7-10 days. Thin seedlings to 25-30cm apart. Can also sow in succession every 2-3 weeks for continuous supply. Keep soil consistently moist. Heart formation over 8-10 weeks. Harvest by cutting at base when head is firm and full. Stores well at 0-5°C for several weeks.

name: Verbena
notes: Annual verbena with fine feathery foliage and purple flowers. Dainty compact plants ideal for containers.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in spring on seed compost, lightly cover. Germination 7-14 days at 18-21°C. Prick out into trays. Harden off and plant out after last frost, 20-30cm apart. Drought tolerant once established. Deadhead regularly for continuous flowering. Ideal for bedding and containers.

name: Forget Me Not
notes: Alpine forget-me-not with rose-pink delicate flowers. Biennial or short-lived perennial for borders.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in late winter on surface of seed compost, lightly press in. Germination 7-14 days. Prick out into trays. Harden off and plant out 20cm apart. Self-seeds readily. Biennial producing flowers in second year. Early flowering starts from mid-spring.

name: Soapwort
notes: Rock garden perennial with pink flowers. Trailing habit suitable for alpine troughs and borders.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in spring on well-draining seed compost, lightly cover. Germination 7-14 days. Prick out into individual pots. Plant out in rock garden or border 30cm apart. Drought tolerant once established. Trailing perennial producing pink flowers for many years.

name: California Poppy
notes: Eschscholzia californica with brilliant red flowers. Annual poppy with delicate tissue-paper petals.
growing_instructions: Sow direct outdoors in spring, barely cover seeds. Germination 7-10 days. Thin to 20cm apart. Prefers poor to moderately fertile soil. Drought tolerant once established. Self-seeds readily. Flowers close at night and on cloudy days. Deadhead regularly for continuous flowering.

name: Lychnis
notes: Vibrant rose-pink flowers on compact plants. Perennial campion variety with long flowering period.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in spring on surface of seed compost, lightly cover. Germination 7-14 days. Prick out into individual pots. Plant out after last frost, 30cm apart in well-drained soil. Prefers sunny position. Deadhead spent flowers to encourage more blooms. Perennial producing flowers for many years.

name: Marigold
notes: Mexican marigold Tagetes tenuifolia mix with colours including orange, red and yellow. Delicate foliage and prolific flowering.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in spring on seed compost surface without covering. Germination 7-10 days at 18-21°C. Prick out when large enough to handle. Harden off and plant out after last frost, 20cm apart. Drought tolerant once established. Deadhead for prolonged flowering.

name: Marigold
notes: African marigold producing huge orange flowers on tall plants. Impressive height and bold colour for borders.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in spring on seed compost surface. Germination 7-10 days at 18-21°C. Prick out when large enough. Harden off and plant out after last frost, 45-60cm apart. Tall varieties may need support. Deadhead spent flowers regularly.

name: Marigold
notes: Dwarf French marigold producing compact plants with abundant golden flowers. Tagetes variety ideal for borders and containers.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in spring on surface of seed compost, do not cover. Germination 7-10 days at 18-21°C. Prick out when large enough. Harden off and plant out after last frost, 30cm apart. Water moderately. Deadhead regularly to encourage more flowers.

name: Nemesia
notes: Annual flower with clustered blooms in vibrant orange. Nemesia strumosa variety produces abundant flowers throughout summer.
growing_instructions: Sow indoors in early spring on seed compost, do not cover. Germination 7-14 days. Prick out into trays when large enough. Harden off and plant out after last frost. Space 15cm apart. Water regularly and deadhead to prolong flowering.
